title: Love Your Block Program Year Two Updates
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: January 22, 2026
TO: Honorable Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Monica Kirch, Director
Cori Fisher, Housing Manager
DEPT: Housing and Neighborhood Development
SUBJECT: Love Your Block Program Year Two Updates
This memo provides an overview of the second year of the Love Your Block grant program within the Housing and Neighborhood Development (HAND) Department.

Due to the success of Year 1, the City received Year 2 funding from Bloomberg's Center for Public Innovation, enabling the planning of additional community events to further support Love Your Block project implementation and resident engagement. For the 2026 program year, Ward 1 was selected as the area of focus, in coordination with Code Enforcement based on a high concentration of residential code violations, including home-related concerns and debris.

In 2026, the Love Your Block program will include two application and funding rounds, each serving a distinct purpose. The first grant cycle, referred to as the CommUNITY Grant, will open on February 13, 2026 and close on March 31, 2026. The CommUNITY Grants will support community-driven projects within Ward 1 that beautify shared spaces, promote neighborhood cleanups, and bring residents together, continuing the momentum and spirit of last year's initiatives while addressing key issues in Ward 1.

Following the application period, a subcommittee of staff members will review and score all submissions in April, with grant awards announced on April 22, 2026. Selected projects will move into the implementation phase from late April through the end of September, allowing sufficient time for coordination, execution, and completion.

The second application round will open on May 1, 2026, and will accept applications on a rolling basis until available funding is exhausted. The second application round will focus on improvements to the exterior of individual homes within Ward 1, including yard enhancements, fence painting, planting, and similar projects.

Based on rubric criteria that evaluate project scale and impact, the establishment of two distinct grant categories allows the Love Your Block program to support both larger-scale neighborhood initiatives and individual home improvements.

fields.authors: Monica Kirch, Director; Cori Fisher, Housing Manager
fields.tldr: The City of Reno's Love Your Block program is entering its second year, funded by Bloomberg's Center for Public Innovation, with Ward 1 selected as the focus area due to high concentrations of residential code violations. The 2026 program features two grant rounds: the CommUNITY Grant for neighborhood beautification and community events, and a second round for individual home exterior improvements. Applications for the CommUNITY Grant open February 13 and close March 31, 2026, with awards announced April 22. The second round opens May 1, 2026, on a rolling basis until funds are exhausted.
fields.key_stats: Two grant funding rounds in 2026; Ward 1 selected as focus area based on high concentration of residential code violations; CommUNITY Grant open February 13–March 31, 2026; awards announced April 22, 2026; implementation phase runs late April through end of September; second round opens May 1, 2026 on a rolling basis.
fields.key_decisions: Ward 1 selected as the 2026 program focus area in coordination with Code Enforcement; two distinct grant categories established to support both larger neighborhood initiatives and individual home improvements; rolling application basis for second grant round until funding is exhausted.
fields.action_items: Open CommUNITY Grant applications on February 13, 2026; close CommUNITY Grant applications on March 31, 2026; subcommittee to review and score submissions in April 2026; announce grant awards on April 22, 2026; begin project implementation late April through end of September 2026; open second application round on May 1, 2026 on a rolling basis.

dates: Late April through end of September 2026 (implementation phase)
people: Monica Kirch, Director, Housing and Neighborhood Development
Cori Fisher, Housing Manager, Housing and Neighborhood Development
